title: rhododendron mariae subsp. flumineum
intro: Rhododendron mariae subsp. flumineum, also known as the mountain azalea, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the heath family, Ericaceae. The roots and leaves of the plant are used in traditional medicine, particularly in Korean and Chinese herbalism, for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rhododendron mariae subsp. flumineum include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral activities. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
